Title: Changes in cell wall composition due to a pectin biosynthesis enzyme GAUT10 impact root growth

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: indole-3-butyric acid (IBA)-derived auxin pool / indole-3-acetic acid (IAA)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls root meristem size and primary root growth via auxin homeostasis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: tob1-3 suppresses the short-root phenotype of gaut10-3 and is epistatic to GAUT10 for IBA response; altered TOB1 expression links GAUT10-dependent cell wall status to IBA/IAA metabolism
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TOB1 transcript is elevated in gaut10-3 roots. 2. tob1-3 and gaut10-3 are insensitive to exogenous IBA, while gaut10-3 responds normally to IAA. 3. The gaut10-3 tob1-3 double mutant resembles tob1-3 for IBA response and suppresses the gaut10-3 short-root phenotype, indicating epistasis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; genetic interaction/epistasis analysis with gaut10-3 tob1-3 double mutant; IBA and IAA root growth assays
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: TOB1 transcript is upregulated in gaut10-3 roots relative to Col-0
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Sucrose-limited medium (15 mM sucrose); exogenous 10 µM IBA or 10 µM IAA treatment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene because it functions as a transporter rather than a biosynthetic enzyme or transcription factor in this study; source and applied species are the same.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GAUT family galacturonosyltransferase; pectin biosynthesis enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Pectin biosynthesis / cell wall polysaccharide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream polymer biosynthesis step in cell wall pectin assembly
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for normal root pectin composition; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Galacturonic acid-containing pectic polysaccharides / HG-linked products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers galacturonic acid from UDP-GalA into pectic polysaccharides during cell wall biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for formation of the structural polysaccharide matrix that influences auxin metabolism and root growth
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: pectin polymers enriched in galacturonic acid, including HG and RG-I-associated wall fractions
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports primary root morphogenesis, root apical meristem maintenance, and cell elongation; cell wall status also impacts auxin metabolism/signaling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of GAUT10 reduces GalA in the pectin fraction and xylose in the hemicellulose fraction, alters HG/RG-I/xylan/xyloglucan epitopes, reduces root meristem size and cell elongation, diminishes DR5 auxin signaling, and alters auxin metabolite homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. gaut10-3 shows a sucrose-dependent short-root phenotype caused by reduced root apical meristem size and shorter epidermal cells. 2. Loss of GAUT10 lowers GalA in the pectin-enriched fraction and xylose in the hemicellulose-enriched fraction and changes multiple HG, RG-I, xylan, and xyloglucan epitopes. 3. gaut10-3 roots display altered auxin-related transcriptomes, reduced DR5:GFP signaling, and changed auxin precursor/conjugate levels, linking cell wall composition to auxin metabolism. 4. Genetic interaction with TOB1 supports a connection between GAUT10-dependent wall status and peroxisome/IBA metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutant analysis (gaut10-3 null); root phenotyping; monosaccharide composition analysis by HPAEC-PAD; glycome profiling by ELISA; RNA-seq; DR5:GFP reporter imaging; LC-MS/MS auxin metabolite profiling; genetic interaction with tob1-3
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: GAUT10 mRNA is enriched in several root stem cell types including quiescent center, columella, and epidermis/lateral root cap initials; GAUT10 protein is auxin downregulated
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Enriched in Arabidopsis root stem cell types including quiescent center, columella, and epidermis/lateral root cap initials
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native gene context; reporter assays used DR5 synthetic promoter with minimal 35S for auxin signaling, not for GAUT10 expression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Sucrose concentration in medium strongly affects phenotype; 0 or 15 mM sucrose reveals short-root phenotype, 30 mM sucrose rescues; exogenous IBA/IAA assays
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Primary root cell wall, especially pectin- and hemicellulose-enriched wall fractions
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes reduced root growth, smaller meristem, and impaired cell elongation; altered auxin signaling and stress-related transcriptomic shifts
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 3' mRNA-seq, NCBI BioProject PRJNA694693; auxin metabolite profiling by LC-MS/MS; glycome profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Recombinant GAUT10 was previously reported to have low catalytic activity with UDP-GalA, suggesting a specialized role in synthesizing unique HG or HG linked to RG-I; in this study the causal focus is on root cell wall composition and downstream auxin/peroxisome phenotypes.
